pattern: Operators are paid per power of x, so walk the target through its base-x digits and at each level either spend that many copies of the current power or overshoot to the next power and subtract back, keeping the cheaper branch.
hints: A term that is x raised to the k-th power costs more operators the higher k is, while a single x/x term gives you the value one.
Process the target digit by digit in base x: at each power you can supply the remainder directly or borrow one of the next power and subtract the shortfall.
Recurse on the quotient while the per-unit cost grows with the power level; at level k each copy costs k operators (and the lowest level uses x/x at cost two), then subtract one for the leading term.

edge_inputs.description: mixed powers and a division term
an exact power needs only multiplications
target equals x itself, zero operators
target of one is a single division
target just below a power favors borrowing

stress.description: random base and target across the allowed ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    x = rng.randint(2, 100)
    target = rng.randint(1, 200000000)
    return [x, target]
stress.note: recursion depth bounded by log_x(target), always fast
